UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents the profile information for an authorized user within the CONEX Gateway application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and communication.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

lastLogin.description: Timestamp of the user's last successful login.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

roleIds.description: References to Roles assigned to this user. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Role)

Role.title: Role
Role.description: Defines a role within the system, used for Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Role entity.

description.description: A brief description of the role's purpose and permissions.

permissions.description: A list of specific permission strings associated with this role (e.g., 'read_content', 'manage_users').

Content.title: Content
Content.description: Represents exclusive media content or resources accessible within the private network.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Content entity.

description.description: A summary or brief description of the content.

url.description: The URL or path where the actual content resource is located.

publishDate.description: The date and time when the content was made available.

creatorUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created or uploaded this content. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Content)

requiredRoleIds.description: References to Roles required to access this content. (Relationship: Content N:N Role)

tags.description: Keywords or categories associated with the content for search and AI curation.

UserPreference.title: UserPreference
UserPreference.description: Stores user-specific preferences used by the AI-Powered Content Curator Tool.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserPreference entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile for whom these preferences apply. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserPreference)

preferredContentTypes.description: A list of content types the user prefers (e.g., ['article', 'video']).

preferredTopics.description: A list of topics or subjects the user is interested in (e.g., ['cybersecurity', 'AI ethics']).

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp when these user preferences were last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`{userId}`) corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID. Access for writes (create, update, delete) is restricted to the owner of the profile (`request.auth.uid == userId`). Read access is typically granted to the owner and potentially to system administrators or for critical authorization lookups (e.g., to retrieve `roleIds`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Defines global system roles and their associated permissions. This collection is primarily read-only for application logic and security rules, with write access highly restricted to system administrators. Includes `id`, `name`, `description`, and `permissions`.

params.name: roleId
params.description: The unique identifier for a system role.

definition.description: Stores exclusive media content and resources. Access control is managed via the `requiredRoleIds` field, which denormalizes the necessary authorization context into each document. This enables robust and atomic role-based access checks directly within security rules, supporting secure `list` operations. Also includes `creatorUserId` for auditing.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a piece of exclusive content.

definition.description: Stores user-specific preferences for the AI-Powered Content Curator Tool. This is a 1:1 relationship where the document ID (`{userId}`) matches `request.auth.uid`, ensuring private, owner-only access for reads and writes. The `userId` field within the document also redundantly confirms ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid` and the document's `userId` field.

firestore.reasoning:
The CONEX Gateway application's Firestore structure is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling, ensuring secure, scalable, and debuggable security rules.

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **User Profiles (`/userProfiles/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored at a top-level collection where the document ID (`{userId}`) directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. This allows for simple, direct path-based ownership for profile management (`read`, `update` for self) and eliminates the need for `get()` calls to verify ownership in many common scenarios.
2.  **User Preferences (`/userPreferences/{userId}`):** Similar to user profiles, user preferences are stored in a dedicated top-level collection, with the document ID matching `request.auth.uid`. This ensures that a user can only read or modify their own preferences without requiring any `get()` operations for authorization.
3.  **Content (`/content/{contentId}`):** Each content document includes a `requiredRoleIds` array. This is a critical denormalization strategy. Instead of security rules having to `get()` the content's parent or a related configuration to determine access, all necessary authorization context (which roles are needed) is directly present within the content document itself. A user's roles (from `/userProfiles/{request.auth.uid}.roleIds`) can be checked directly against `resource.data.requiredRoleIds` in the rule, enabling atomic access checks for individual content items and eliminating `get()` calls for content authorization.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Structural Segregation for `Content`:** The `/content` collection adheres to homogeneous security posture. All documents within this collection require role-based access defined by their `requiredRoleIds` field. This enables secure `list` operations because clients are expected to query for content based on their assigned roles (e.g., `db.collection('content').where('requiredRoleIds', 'array-contains-any', userRoles)`). The Firestore rules can then confidently validate that *every* document attempting to be read or returned by such a query indeed satisfies the `requiredRoleIds` condition relative to the requesting user's roles. Since `requiredRoleIds` is directly on the document, rules can enforce this without needing to filter on server-side `get()` operations, which Firestore rules explicitly do not do.
2.  **Path-Based Ownership:** For `/userProfiles/{userId}` and `/userPreferences/{userId}`, `list` operations are typically constrained by the `userId` in the path (e.g., listing only *your own* preferences). This inherently supports QAPs as the query is already constrained by the authenticated user's ID, which the rules can easily verify.

**DBAC (No Custom Claims):** User roles are explicitly stored in the `/userProfiles/{userId}` documents as the `roleIds` array, and the definitions of these roles are in the `/roles/{roleId}` collection. Firebase security rules will leverage `request.auth.uid` to retrieve the current user's profile and subsequently check their `roleIds` against the `requiredRoleIds` on content documents, fully adhering to DBAC principles.
